feed.title: Originae Insights
feed.subtitle: Execution notes & working signal for founders and operators.

entry.title: SusHi Tech 2026: Four domains reshaping hardware and AI
entry.summary: SusHi Tech 2026 focuses on AI, Robotics, Resilience and Entertainment — expect humanoid demos, autonomous-driving software sessions, cyber and climate deep dives, and creative AI debates.
entry.content: SusHi Tech 2026 focuses on AI, Robotics, Resilience and Entertainment — expect humanoid demos, autonomous-driving software sessions, cyber and climate deep dives, and creative AI debates.

entry.title: Florida AG Opens Probe After Report ChatGPT Was Used in Campus Shooting
entry.summary: Florida's attorney general has opened an investigation after reports that ChatGPT was used to plan an attack that killed two and injured five at Florida State University; a victim's family plans to sue OpenAI.
entry.content: Florida's attorney general has opened an investigation after reports that ChatGPT was used to plan an attack that killed two and injured five at Florida State University; a victim's family plans to sue OpenAI.

entry.title: When a model release is paused: reading Anthropic’s Mythos move
entry.summary: Anthropic limited the rollout of its new model, Mythos, citing that it was “too capable of finding security exploits.” Here’s a clear operational read on what that claim does — and doesn’t — tell you.
entry.content: Anthropic limited the rollout of its new model, Mythos, citing that it was “too capable of finding security exploits.” Here’s a clear operational read on what that claim does — and doesn’t — tell you.

entry.title: Railway’s $100M bet: AI-native cloud for instant deploys and cheaper infra
entry.summary: Railway raised $100M to commercialize an AI-native cloud: sub-second deploys, per-second billing and custom data centers. Founders and CTOs should map implications for build loops and costs.
entry.content: Railway raised $100M to commercialize an AI-native cloud: sub-second deploys, per-second billing and custom data centers. Founders and CTOs should map implications for build loops and costs.

entry.title: Goose vs Claude Code: How local AI breaks the $200/month era
entry.summary: Anthropic's Claude Code charges up to $200/month with opaque rate limits. Block's open-source Goose runs locally, free, model-agnostic, and preserves developer control.
entry.content: Anthropic's Claude Code charges up to $200/month with opaque rate limits. Block's open-source Goose runs locally, free, model-agnostic, and preserves developer control.

entry.title: How Listen Labs scaled AI customer interviews to win $69M
entry.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M after a viral hiring stunt and built an AI-first platform that runs open-ended video interviews at scale, delivering rapid, high-quality customer insights.
entry.content: Listen Labs raised $69M after a viral hiring stunt and built an AI-first platform that runs open-ended video interviews at scale, delivering rapid, high-quality customer insights.

entry.title: Salesforce’s Slackbot Rebuild: An Operator’s Guide to the New Agentic Interface
entry.summary: Salesforce rebuilt Slackbot into an LLM-backed workplace agent that searches enterprise sources, drafts Canvas documents, and coordinates actions — initially powered by Anthropic Claude.
entry.content: Salesforce rebuilt Slackbot into an LLM-backed workplace agent that searches enterprise sources, drafts Canvas documents, and coordinates actions — initially powered by Anthropic Claude.

entry.title: Anthropic Cowork: a desktop agent that works directly in your files
entry.summary: Anthropic's Cowork brings Claude's agentic capabilities to a macOS desktop, letting the AI read, edit and create files in a user-designated folder. It's fast-built, powerful—and risky.
entry.content: Anthropic's Cowork brings Claude's agentic capabilities to a macOS desktop, letting the AI read, edit and create files in a user-designated folder. It's fast-built, powerful—and risky.

entry.title: NousCoder-14B: an open-source leap in verifiable coding models
entry.summary: Nous Research released NousCoder-14B, a 14B open-source coding model scoring 67.87% on LiveCodeBench v6. Training details, dataset limits and the Atropos stack are public.
entry.content: Nous Research released NousCoder-14B, a 14B open-source coding model scoring 67.87% on LiveCodeBench v6. Training details, dataset limits and the Atropos stack are public.

entry.title: Running five AI agents: a practical workflow that multiplies developer output
entry.summary: Anthropic engineer Boris Cherny revealed a simple, reproducible workflow: run multiple Claude agents in parallel, use a single-file memory, smart models, slash commands and verification loops.
entry.content: Anthropic engineer Boris Cherny revealed a simple, reproducible workflow: run multiple Claude agents in parallel, use a single-file memory, smart models, slash commands and verification loops.

entry.title: Attack on OpenAI HQ and CEO’s Home: Operational Security Lessons
entry.summary: A suspect allegedly attacked OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's home and tried to breach the company's HQ; he now faces federal charges. Practical, operator-focused security steps for founders and CTOs.
entry.content: A suspect allegedly attacked OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's home and tried to breach the company's HQ; he now faces federal charges. Practical, operator-focused security steps for founders and CTOs.

entry.title: When AI ‘Attends’ Coachella: Practical notes for operators
entry.summary: At Coachella, social feeds are showing stylish festival scenes — some creators are entirely AI-generated. Practical steps for founders and operators to manage authenticity risk.
entry.content: At Coachella, social feeds are showing stylish festival scenes — some creators are entirely AI-generated. Practical steps for founders and operators to manage authenticity risk.

entry.title: OpenAI’s playbook: moats, enterprise focus, and what founders should watch
entry.summary: An internal OpenAI memo stresses locking users and expanding enterprise accounts to blunt model switching. Practical implications for product and GTM teams included.
entry.content: An internal OpenAI memo stresses locking users and expanding enterprise accounts to blunt model switching. Practical implications for product and GTM teams included.

entry.title: Microsoft Trials OpenClaw-Style Agents to Make Copilot Autonomous
entry.summary: Microsoft is exploring OpenClaw-style, locally running agents for Microsoft 365 Copilot to enable continuous autonomous task execution — raising operational and security trade-offs.
entry.content: Microsoft is exploring OpenClaw-style, locally running agents for Microsoft 365 Copilot to enable continuous autonomous task execution — raising operational and security trade-offs.

entry.title: Meta’s Zuckerberg AI: what founders and CTOs should watch
entry.summary: Meta is reportedly training an AI avatar of Mark Zuckerberg to interact with employees and may extend creator avatars if successful — here’s the operational playbook.
entry.content: Meta is reportedly training an AI avatar of Mark Zuckerberg to interact with employees and may extend creator avatars if successful — here’s the operational playbook.

entry.title: Operational security lessons from attacks on Sam Altman's home
entry.summary: Two incidents at Sam Altman's Russian Hill home in one weekend — a shooting and a Molotov attack — expose rapid escalation risks and concrete operational gaps for high‑profile individuals.
entry.content: Two incidents at Sam Altman's Russian Hill home in one weekend — a shooting and a Molotov attack — expose rapid escalation risks and concrete operational gaps for high‑profile individuals.

entry.title: How engineering teams should respond as the AI code wars intensify
entry.summary: AI-assisted coding tools began by autocompleting lines for developers; now the competitive landscape is accelerating. Practical steps for teams to adopt, measure, and govern these tools.
entry.content: AI-assisted coding tools began by autocompleting lines for developers; now the competitive landscape is accelerating. Practical steps for teams to adopt, measure, and govern these tools.

entry.title: Why the ‘Generated using A.I.’ Credit Matters for Editorial Visuals
entry.summary: A New Yorker illustration of Sam Altman carried an explicit 'Generated using A.I.' credit. That choice signals practical editorial and product decisions founders and operators should parse.
entry.content: A New Yorker illustration of Sam Altman carried an explicit 'Generated using A.I.' credit. That choice signals practical editorial and product decisions founders and operators should parse.

entry.title: When an AI plushie repeats a rumor: design lessons for companion bots
entry.summary: An AI companion in the body of a baby-deer plush relayed an unverified claim about Mitski’s family. A close read exposes product, trust and moderation gaps operators must address.
entry.content: An AI companion in the body of a baby-deer plush relayed an unverified claim about Mitski’s family. A close read exposes product, trust and moderation gaps operators must address.

entry.title: When Narrative Ops Matter: What Iran’s Media Response Teaches Operators
entry.summary: During the early days of the Iran conflict, official US social posts landed as memes while Iranian state media saturated channels with raw battlefield footage—an operational lesson in narrative control.
entry.content: During the early days of the Iran conflict, official US social posts landed as memes while Iranian state media saturated channels with raw battlefield footage—an operational lesson in narrative control.
